summary: The United Kingdom government has committed £2 billion over four years to quantum computing research, aiming to encourage companies to build prototype systems and move towards commercial deployment within the next decade, according to the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology. Targeted sectors include financial services, quantum navigation, energy, and pharmaceuticals. The initiative will launch the ProQure: Scaling UK Quantum Computing programme and fund up to 100 internships in the field. Notable developments include Infleqtion delivering a 100-qubit machine at the National Quantum Computing Centre and IonQ partnering with Cambridge University on a 256-qubit computer. The government claims quantum technology could increase productivity by 7% over twenty years, creating over 100,000 jobs and generating £212 billion in economic impact.
